summary: Monster Playing in the Band and soaring Sugar Magnolia
blurb: The Fox Theatre show delivers a high‑energy first set packed with tight jams and a second set that leans into song‑focused brilliance. Highlights include an epic Playing In The Band, a soaring Sugar Magnolia, and a marathon Half‑Step/Not Fade Away sequence.
review: October 17, 1972 at St. Louis’ Fox Theatre captures the Dead in top form. The first set is jam‑heavy, launching from a crisp Bird Song into a blistering Playing In The Band that many reviewers call “monster.” Jerry’s solos are described as “great,” “on fire,” and “mind‑blowing noodling,” while Phil rides the waves of the jams with “on fire” bass work. Keith Godchaux shines on the GSET, adding texture that lifts the whole performance. The second set rolls into a legendary Mississippi Half‑Step Uptown Toodeloo segueing into an extended Not Fade Away jam that spirals through Good Times Bad Times before returning to the classic two‑beat groove—an “epic” moment praised across the board. Sugar Magnolia soars with bright vocals and tight rhythm, and the encore rounds it out with Uncle John’s Band and a rollicking Johnny B. Goode. The only blemish noted is a brief cut in Black Peter, but overall the show is cohesive, high‑octane, and a must‑listen for any Deadhead.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All recorded commentary is overwhelmingly positive, praising Jerry’s fireworks, Phil’s locked‑in bass, Keith’s standout keyboards, and the epic jam sequences. No consistent negative remarks about performance quality appear; only a minor issue with Black Peter is noted.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with clear, crisp mix and no major issues; showcases the high‑energy performance in full.

key_highlights: Monster Playing In The Band jam that drives the first set.
Epic Mississippi Half‑Step > Not Fade Away marathon with Good Times Bad Times detour.
Soaring Sugar Magnolia leading into a tight second‑set flow.
Crystal‑clear soundboard recording preserving every nuance.

band_performance.Jerry: Solos are described as great, on fire, and mind‑blowing noodling; tone praised throughout the show.
band_performance.Phil: Bass work is on fire and in great form, serving as a star of several jams.
band_performance.Bob: Delivers tasty licks and strong vocal delivery, with only a minor quirk of quasi‑falsetto noted on one song.
band_performance.Keith: Stands out on the GSET version, providing stellar keyboard textures that lift the jam sections.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
